We will wow your world! The
**CNC Manufacturing Engineer**
is responsible for developing, optimizing, and sustaining CNC machining processes to support high-precision, high-volume production. The role involves CAD/CAM programming, tooling engineering, equipment capability analysis, and continuous improvement. Position Summary:

A process engineer at Crane Resistoflex is responsible for supporting production through daily trouble shooting and root cause analysis to ensure processes are safe, meet quality and throughput requirements and make the work easier for the operators. This requires hands on involvement and working on the shop floor. In addition to this support, they will be responsible for design and implementation of tooling and/or process changes to improve manufacturing processes. They will

We are seeking a Tooling Engineer responsible for designing, developing, and managing tooling for manufacturing processes. This role ensures tools meet specifications, quality standards, schedule, and budget requirements, supporting both internal teams and external suppliers.

**Key Responsibilities:**

- Design and maintain tooling layouts, BOMs, and documentation in accordance with standards and specifications.
- Support purchasing with tool quoting, procurement, and supplier management.

Who are Composite Resources and Re:Build Manufacturing?

Composite Resources, a Re:Build Manufacturing company, is an innovative, rapidly growing manufacturer of composite components and tooling located in Rock Hill, SC. Our constantly evolving portfolio of projects includes parts and assemblies for clients in the aerospace and defense industries. At Composite Resources, we embrace a culture defined by teamwork, ownership, and accountability.

Re:Build Manufacturing New Kensington is part of the Re:Build family of companies, located in New Kensington, PA. Capabilities in a wide variety of industries and materials allow our unique value proposition to bring exciting and advanced programs back to American Manufacturing.

## About P&G Manufacturing

P&G Manufacturing is all about safety, quality, and productivity. We package and palletize products ready for shipment, but this goes far beyond traditional warehousing. Our operations include state-of-the-art robotics, automation, zero-touch systems, and zero waste to landfill capabilities. Our team of analysts, engineers, leaders, and managers ensures the excellence of our 65 iconic, trusted brands throughout P&G's global end-to-end supply chain.
